location.heading: How to Convert PowerPoint to eLearning in Minutes

findings.sentence: ✋ Raise your hand if you’ve suffered [death by PowerPoint](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Iwpi1Lm6dFo) — decks with 47 bullets per slide, 9-point font, and diagrams that require a microscope.

meta.anchor: short, segmented content works better than one long run

location.heading: Why do most PowerPoints fail as self-paced training?

findings.sentence: People dip in between meetings, while multitasking, and with limited attention, so [short, segmented content works better than one long run](https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/multimedia-learning/segmenting-principle/37240877DDA0362355ADB39936027982).

meta.anchor: learning takes a hit (the redundancy problem)

location.heading: Why do most PowerPoints fail as self-paced training?

findings.sentence: Dense on-screen text plus narration forces people to read and listen at the same time, and [learning takes a hit (the redundancy problem)](https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/cambridge-handbook-of-multimedia-learning/redundancy-principle-in-multimedia-learning/448A5532008EB4B4BA17DBEB5A421920).

meta.anchor: you add extra mental work that doesn’t help them learn the task

location.heading: Why do most PowerPoints fail as self-paced training?

findings.sentence: When people have to split attention across dense text and visuals, [you add extra mental work that doesn’t help them learn the task](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/BF00375135).
